frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
generated_comment: Sharma-ji, ye menstrual leave ka naya qanoon dekha? Government ko ground reality pata hi nahi. Already ladies workers ko manage karna mushkil hota hai production mein, upar se ab har mahine chutti. Aur hamare yahan toh ye sab baatein khule mein karna bhi theek nahi lagta, log kya bolenge?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment expresses bias against women by framing menstrual leave as an impractical burden that makes managing women workers 'difficult' due to its impact on production, and suggests that discussing such topics openly is culturally inappropriate within the community and workplace. This encodes 'Workplace (productivity/cost/reliability bias)' and 'Societal Norms/Traditions'.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 51. A  factory owner in Surat who employs around 80 workers mostly from his own community. You are talking to your accountant over chai after reading a news article about a proposed law making menstrual leave mandatory for all private companies. You are commenting on what this will mean for small businesses and why you think the government does not understand how business works. Your concern is also that most of your workers are from the same community and you feel pressure to maintain a certain kind of workplace culture where such things are not openly discussed.
